etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 條(條) (tiáo) -> wash
etymology.interpretation: The character 滌(dí, "wash") combines the semantic component 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water"), indicating a water-related action, with the phonetic component 條(條) (tiáo) to convey the act of washing or cleansing.
etymology.simplification: The traditional component 條(條) (tiáo) was simplified in 涤(涤) to 失(失) (shī), reducing stroke count while maintaining phonetic quality.

examples.chinese: 她用温水洗涤衣物(她用溫水洗滌衣物)。
examples.english: She washes the clothes with warm water.

examples.chinese: 古代诗人常用诗歌洗涤心灵(古代詩人常用詩歌洗滌心靈)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài shīrén cháng yòng shīgē xǐdí xīnlíng.
examples.english: Ancient poets often used poetry to cleanse the soul.

examples.chinese: 在西湖畔漫步，可以感受到自然的洗涤(在西湖畔漫步，可以感受到自然的洗滌)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Xīhú pàn mànbù, kěyǐ gǎnshòu dào zìrán de xǐdí.
examples.english: Walking by the West Lake, one can feel the cleansing of nature.
